channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.thesixthaxis.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.thesixthaxis.com

item.title: Gang of Dragon appears to have been cancelled and the studio closed
item.content:encoded:
Gang of Dragon was announced at The Game Awards 2025 from Toshihiro Nagoshi, the former general director of Ryu Ga Gotoko Studio, who had left Sega to run a new start-up called Nagoshi Studios.

Nothing more has been heard of the game since then,  but the YouTube channel for the studio has been shut down along with the official website. Studio co-head, Daisuke Sato, has now set his bio to “Ex-Nagoshi Studios” and the Google Business listing for the studio, which is based in Tokyo Ebisu district, is marked as “Permanently closed”.

In March this year, Bloomberg reported that NetEase, who were funding the game, refused a payout of $44 million to finish the game.

No official announcement has been made about its cancellation, and the game is still listed on Steam, so it seems they have forgotten to remove it when they closed the door for the last time.

Source: YouTube / ResetEra



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/17/gang-of-dragon-appears-to-have-been-cancelled-and-the-studio-closed/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Report: Redundancies at Microsoft owned Zenimax have begun
item.content:encoded:
The co-founder 3D Realms, George Broussard, is reporting that Microsoft has started swinging the axe at Zenimax, the umbrella company that includes Bethesda (Fallout), ID (Quake), Arkane (Dishonored), Machine Games (Wolfenstein) and Zenimax Online (Elder Scrolls Online).

He replied to a tweet that suggested “Anyone not working on Fallout or Elder Scrolls is out” with “Oh is that public now? That’s exactly what I heard a few days ago.”

There were suggestions that at least Arkane was negotiating for the chance to go independent.

Reports have also emerged that Ninja Theory, the studio behind the Hellblade series (including the very, very recently announced Senua) is set to be shut down by Microsoft.

Sources told The Verge that staff were informed on Monday about the studio’s coming closure, though they are hoping to find a buyer for the studio and avoid being shut down entirely. Microsoft did not immediately respond to The Verge’s reporting.

Things are moving very quickly following the initial reports about Xbox game studios potentially shutting down, with Ninja Theory mentioned alongside Compulsion Games and Double Fine as studios that are facing closure. Kotaku reported that Microsoft is set to close down Compulsion, while Bloomberg reported that all three are bargaining for their future away from Xbox.

All of this is coming just days after a swell of positivity for the Xbox brand at Summer Game Fest. The company had reversed the full cross-platform policy during the showcase, confirming that Gears of War: E-Day and Clockwork Revolution are to be full Xbox console exclusives, but just days later, she was revealing a massive slide in profitability and saying that Xbox needs a deep business reset.

Source: Reddit



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/17/report-redundancies-at-microsoft-owned-zenimax-have-begun/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Black Ops and Black Ops II are being ported to PS4 and PS5
item.content:encoded:
Treyarch have announced, rather quietly via a tweet, that the original Call of Duty: Black Ops and Call of Duty: Black Ops II are being ported to PlayStation 4 and 5.

Now I know what you are thinking, Microsoft own Activision, so why on earth are they porting old COD games just to PlayStation 5? Well, you can already play them on Xbox backwards compatibility, and Treyarch are not handling ports. Instead,  Iron Galaxy, a team who have previously worked with Sony porting Uncharted and The Last of Us to PC, will be doing the work.

It’s also worth emphasising that these are being announced as ports, not remakes or remasters,. Black Ops and Black Ops II will be available in July and will feature the full package of a single player campaign, multiplayer, and Zombies modes. The price was not revealed.

It’s a rather odd announcement, given that Xbox is trying to claw back exclusives and that you can’t play the games on current-gen PlayStation, it does mean BLOPS and BLOPS 2 are sort of exclusive to Xbox.

Source: X



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/17/black-ops-and-black-ops-ii-are-being-ported-to-ps4-and-ps5/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
